Read More Do I Need to Brush My Teeth More Often if I Have Braces. You definitely need to brush your teeth every morning and every evening when in braces, and a water flosser/water pik is highly recommended to get in between your teeth. It does take a little longer to clean your teeth correctly when you have braces on because it is imperative that patients have great hygiene while wearing braces. This is to avoid developing stains around the braces.

For patients who play an instrument, your embouchure may change a bit, but you will adjust within only a couple of weeks. Band directors recommend that you do NOT get braces put on or taken off before a big concert or UIL Top McKinney Orthodontics competition. Please make sure and keep your orthodontist in the loop of any big competitions coming up while you are in braces.

Read More If I Have Braces, Do I Still Need Dental Checkups Every Six Months. Yes, you definitely need to continue your six month cleaning and checkups with your general dentist while wearing braces. The dentist will be able to clean your teeth really well while you have your braces on with their special dental cleaning tools. The orthodontist may ask you to visit your dentist more frequently (every three to four months) depending on how well the patient does with their oral hygiene.

It is not recommended to make any major orthodontic adjustments right before any big competition for a band, orchestra, or choir performance. How can I take care of my teeth while wearing braces. There are several ways to take care of your teeth while in braces. One, it’s essential to brush and floss every day. Second, it’s imperative to stay away from chewy, gooey, and sticky foods, so your braces won’t become loose or damaged.

When the patient gets their braces on, their teeth may be a bit sore for approximately a week as their teeth and gums get used to something new on them. However, the patient can take pain relievers, such as Tylenol or Advil, to help alleviate this pain. Patients generally come about every 8-10 weeks for an orthodontic adjustment to their braces so that Top McKinney Orthodontics soreness may last for only a couple of days after each adjustment, and then all should be well.

Third, don’t ever chew on ice while wearing braces, and stay away from biting directly into anything hard, such as apples or corn on the cob. You can still eat these items, but be sure and cut up apples into slices and cut corn off the cob. Fourth, it is imperative to use the fluoride rinse nightly that you are given at your first appointment when you get your braces put on. Fluoride will guard against cavities or decalcified white spots that can occur on your teeth while wearing braces.

Ask your dentist if they can use a prophy jet at your next cleaning. This is a great way to clean teeth with braces. Read More Will My Braces Interfere With My School Activities Like Sports, Playing an Instrument, or Singing. There are many students in braces that participate in sports, choir(vocal performance), band, etc. Patients will want to be sure and wear a mouthguard when playing sports so if they get hit in the mouth, the braces won’t tear up their lips or cheeks.

When in active clear Top McKinney Orthodontics aligners, it is important to stay away from sugary drinks while wearing your clear-aligners and to not eat in your active clear-aligners. It is also important to clean your clear-aligners every day with toothpaste and water. Read More How do I schedule my next appointment. Your first orthodontic appointments will be made after you come in for your initial consultation. Then, most adjustment appointments while in braces are about 8-10 weeks apart.

The usual time for wearing braces is between 18-24 months; however, the patient’s help is critical in determining if they get their braces off during this time frame. Often, rubber bands are worn as part of their treatment, and it is crucial that the patient wears them as instructed, or their treatment time can be longer. Read More Do Braces Hurt. Braces can take a little bit of time to get used to, but they usually do not hurt.

Plus, clear brackets can also stain if the patient drinks a lot of dark-colored drinks, such as sodas, coffee, or tea. So, good oral hygiene is a definite must if a patient wants clear braces. Read More If I Get Braces, How Long Do I Have to Wear Them. Each patient has their own treatment plan, including the length of time they will be in braces.

We look forward to working with you and can’t wait to give you an absolutely beautiful Hollywood Smile.

0% Interest in-house financing Smiles Hollywood Orthodontics allows your family to pay out the cost of your or your child’s orthodontic treatment. In most cases, we do not charge interest so you will not have to be concerned about additional costs. Down payment assistance Down payments run anywhere from $250 to $1250. In many cases, we will allow the down payment to be split into two or three payments depending on the amount.

Flexible spending accounts accepted Many people make use of their flexible spending accounts each year for orthodontic care which is a great tax saving strategy. OBTAINING YOUR INSURANCE INFORMATION When you make your appointment with us, we will ask you for your dental insurance information. Many insurance companies are not mailing dental cards anymore, so you will have to check with your employer’s Human Resources department to get your dental information.

These are several ways that we try to help make orthodontic care possible for our top Mckinney Orthodontics patients: ORTHODONTIC FINANCING OPTIONS Payment options provide assistance with investing in your new smile that will last a lifetime. Affordable Payment Plans Everyone’s finances are different and payment plans should reflect that. We will work with you to create a flexible plan that is within your budget including a down payment you can afford.

In other words, if you have $1,000 in orthodontic insurance coverage, that is how much you have for the lifetime for that individual. The only way you may get more orthodontic insurance is if you have a job change and then you may get more orthodontic coverage. As a courtesy to you, we will apply that towards your balance on your contract and we will file the insurance for you. Insurance will then pay towards your orthodontics either monthly or quarterly during the treatment.

We will need the subscriber’s name and date of birth, as well as the subscriber’s employer and ID number and Group number. As a courtesy to you, we will verify your insurance and see how much orthodontic insurance you have available. One important thing to understand before your appointment with top Mckinney Orthodontics insurance is that, unlike medical and dental insurance that starts over each year with its’ benefits; orthodontic insurance is a one-time, lifetime benefit per covered individual.

Many people will use their FSA and/or HSA cards for the monthly payments, which is great. Just let us know. PAID IN FULL DISCOUNT If you would like to pay in full for your treatment then this is just another way that we try to help our patients financially.

During this phase, we are able to correct many skeletal issues by using a few different appliances, such as expanders, face masks, headgear or the Herbst device. This phase is generally done in girls around the ages of 7-9 years old and in boys around the ages of 8-10 years old. Not everyone will need a Phase 1 treatment. Then, a few years later, Phase II is done, which is full braces (around age 12 to 14 years old).

We also give discounts for those in the military, teachers, nurses/first responders and pastors. Also, we offer a top Mckinney Orthodontics homeschool discount if you are willing to make your appointments during school hours only. These are hours between 9am and 2pm. Smiles Hollywood Orthodontics does run specials from time to time as well. FLEXIBILITY IN CASE OF FINANCIAL CRISIS We are flexible in case of a crisis.

If you do have a financial crisis, such as a job loss, etc, please let us know, and oftentimes, we can work with you and possibly make your payments a bit smaller for a few months, until you are back on your feet again financially. We understand that life happens and we will do what we can to help you in case of a financial problem. We are on your team and want everyone to be able to afford a beautiful top Mckinney Orthodontics smile.

When you sign your contract for your treatment plan, please let the top Mckinney Orthodontics treatment coordinator know that you want to be set up on an easy pay plan. All you have to do is give us a credit card that you want your monthly payment to go on and you even get to pick the day of the month that you want the payment to be put on your credit card. By doing this, you don’t have to think about making your monthly payment, as it is automatically drafted each month for you.

We look forward to working with you and your family at Smiles Hollywood Orthodontics. Please feel free to contact us with any questions that you might have. ZERO % FINANCING One great service we offer is 0% in-house financing provided with NO INTEREST. This is available for most of our patients.

Insurance usually does not pay a lump sum amount. If you do change jobs and orthodontic insurance coverage plans change, please let us know right away and we will file a work in progress, most of the time, the new insurance company will pick up where the old insurance left off. We are happy to do this for all of our patients. Please just let us know. top Mckinney Orthodontics has two phases. Phase I is a skeletal phase.

From time to time, we may publish a short video clip on our website or in a short YouTube video compilation. We would love to feature you or your child in some of these Top Mckinney Orthodontics publications if you are okay with it. We will never use your last name on social media. The Covid-19 Informed Consent Form must be filled out on THE DAY OF YOUR APPOINTMENT, so please wait to fill them out.

We know that most insurance companies do not send out dental cards anymore. This makes it a bit more difficult for everyone to obtain this information. Still, we need your dental information to verify your insurance to see if you have any Top Mckinney Orthodontics coverage. Please give us all the information you can on the insurance section so we can find your coverage for you. We will need the subscribers name, employer, date of birth, social security number, group number, and ID number.

Some people are allergic to latex, so we have special latex-free gloves that our doctors will wear for those patients who are allergic to latex. Please also remember to give us your email address, phone number, etc. We also ask for your spouses information as well (if you are married). We need the spouse’s information because your orthodontic insurance is often listed under your husband’s information, so we do need their information as well. The next section asks about your insurance information.

It is also excellent if you could provide us with the phone number of your dental insurance carrier. We always try to verify all of your orthodontic coverage before you come into the office to save you time, which is why it is so critical that you fill out all of your forms before you arrive for your visit. Our staff can often be on hold with an insurance company for an hour or more in order to verify your benefits, so let us do this footwork for you.

Then, there is our HIPAA form that ensures your privacy. The HIPAA form states that we will not release any information to anyone outside medical, dental, or insurance company personnel unless you tell us to. The Photo/Video Release form allows our doctors to show your image for teachings and scientific meetings or conferences. Please check yes or no on this page and then sign and date it. We love to showcase some of our awesome patients in advertising or marketing materials.

In that case, we need to know that Top Mckinney Orthodontics information because we will need a note from their cardiologist as to whether or not they need to be premedicated before each of their orthodontic appointments. We are happy to call in pre-meds for you during your appointments if needed. Of course, we also need to know if you have allergies to anything at all.

Also, just a side note on orthodontic insurance coverage, it is very different from medical and dental insurance in that usually, it is a one-time lifetime maximum per patient. For example, if you have $1500 in Top Mckinney Orthodontics coverage, that is the total amount you have for the patients lifetime unless the subscriber takes a new job and gets more coverage then. Next, we ask that you sign the authorization forms stating that the insurance carrier can release benefit information to us.

It also asks for the name and ages of other children and/or siblings. Of course, please let us know if one or more of the siblings is already in treatment with us. We love having multiple family members as our patients. This form also asks how you heard about our office. This form is significantly important to us because we like to know where our patients are coming from so that we have the opportunity to thank anyone who has referred you to us.

So, please let us know if you saw us in a publication, online, if one of your friends is a patient here (we love referrals from our current and past patients. ) or if you saw Dr. Crise at a local school visit. Next, we ask for a Medical History form. Please check Yes or No next to each question. All of this information is very important so we know if extra care needs to be taken with a patient, especially if a medical condition exists. For example, suppose a patient has a heart murmur or defect.

This form not only protects the Top Mckinney Orthodontics staff at Smiles Hollywood Orthodontics from ill people entering our office, but it also allows you, as the patient, to know we are taking every precaution possible to protect our patients from COVID-19. Please carefully answer each of the questions on the form, and please call us ahead of time to reschedule your appointment if you think your child could be ill or if they have been exposed to COVID-19.

Swollen Gums It is very important to keep your teeth and gums healthy during orthodontic treatment. Poor oral hygiene may result in swollen and bleeding gum tissue. Gentle and thorough brushing, rinsing with warm salt water and mouthwash (e. g. , Peroxyl) will help return the gums to a healthy state. Also, use dental floss, Proxabrush or a Waterpik to remove any food or debris that may have become trapped under the gum line. Orthodontic tooth movement may also cause swollen gums.

Pain in the teeth Pain in the gums Swollen facial tissues (cheek or chin area) Swollen Gums Temporomandibular Joint (TMJ) Pain Sore Places in the cheeks or roof of mouth Sore Places on the lips Sore Places on the tongue or floor of mouth Loose bracket Loose bonded appliance (upper or lower expanders) Loose archwire or irritating wire Loose or broken retainers or removable appliances Swallowed parts Unusual movement of your teeth Allergies Wax Pain in the Teeth Sore teeth during orthodontic treatment are normal and results from the forces placed on the teeth to move them.
If it is after hours and an emergency, please call (214) 551-2938.

Pain in the Gums Pain in the gums during orthodontic treatment is not normal. One possible cause is food trapped under a bracket or beneath the gum line. Rinse your mouth several times per day with warm salt water and/or a mouthwash, such as Peroxyl. Brush your teeth and gums with a soft toothbrush and use either dental floss, an interdental toothbrush (Proxabrush) or a Waterpik to dislodge the food.

16 global_colors_info={}][et_pb_text admin_label=Emergency Orthodontic Care _builder_version=4. 27. 0 hover_enabled=0 global_colors_info={} sticky_enabled=0] Emergency orthodontic care, though rare, is sometimes necessary. In the event that something does occur where you need emergency orthodontic care, Smiles Hollywood Orthodontics has someone on call 24 hours per day, every day of the year. If an emergency occurs during office hours, call (972) 529-9700 and someone will assist you.

If the steps above do not help the situation, please alert our office. Temporomandibular Joint (Tmj) Pain Pops, clicks, and discomfort are sometimes felt in the TMJ (jaw joint) while undergoing orthodontic treatment. These symptoms are usuallycome and go because the teeth and jaw structures are moving into better alignment as orthodontic treatment progresses. Please note, if the pain in the TMJ(s) is persistent or severe and you are in need of emergency orthodontic care, call our office.

If you experience severe pain or if the pain lasts longer than a few days, please call our office at 972-529-9700 so that a Smiles Hollywood trained professional can help determine its source. Swollen Facial Tissues (Cheeks or Chin) Any facial swelling should not be ignored especially if combined with fever since this usually indicates an infection. For emergency orthodontic care, please alert our office so Dr. Crise or Dr. Downs can examine the problem, or call your medical doctor immediately.

You or your child is most likely to experience sore teeth for 1 to 7 days after the braces are first placed and 1 to 3 days after a routine orthodontic visit. An anti-inflammatory medication such as ibuprofen (e. g. , Advil, Motrin) is very good at reducing the soreness. Tylenol (Acetaminophen) can also be used for pain. Also, avoid hard or chewy foods while your teeth are sore. If you experience intense pain or if the soreness persists longer than you think is normal, please call our office.

You or your child may need to be seen by Dr. Crise or Dr. Downs to make sure it is just a normal part of the orthodontic treatment. Sore Places Inside the Cheeks or Roof of Mouth Braces and some other orthodontic appliances can sometimes irritate the inside of the cheek or roof of the mouth, especially during the first few days following the placement of braces. A sore spot may develop due to the orthodontic brackets or wires.

To help the sore spot heal, place wax on the area or bracket adjacent to the sore spot and use a warm salt water rinse or Peroxyl rinse three (3) times daily. Please refer to the topic “Wax” at the bottom of this page for complete instructions on the use of wax. If the wax does not help or if the sore spot seems to get worse, please call our office. Sore Places on the Lips During the first week of wearing your braces, your lips may be irritated from the brackets on your front teeth.

Stop turning the loose appliance. Dr. Crise and Dr. Downs recommend not removing the appliance. Keeping it in your mouth will maintain the expansion achieved thus far and will prevent any relapse. It is important to call our office as soon as possible to inquire about scheduling an appointment to repair the loose appliance.

Please refer to the topic “Wax” at the bottom of this page for complete instructions on the use of wax. Note: Loose brackets cannot support the use of elastics. Loose Bonded Appliance (Upper or Lower Expanders) If a bonded appliance comes loose, it can cause irritation and soreness in the soft tissues. A loose orthodontic appliance will not move the teeth to their proper positions. This can result in a delay in treatment progress. Do not try to fix these problems yourself.

Sore Places on the Tongue or Floor of Mouth Some orthodontic appliances may have parts that lie on the surfaces of your teeth toward the tongue or near the floor of the mouth. This is called the lingual surface. These parts may irritate the tongue for several days as you get used to your braces.

If a sore spot develops on your lip, cover the part of your bracket adjacent to the sore spot with wax and lubricate your lips with Vaseline or Chapstick. Please refer to the topic “Wax” at the bottom of this page for complete instructions on the use of wax. If these steps do not help or if the sore spot seems to get worse, please call our Smiles Hollywood office at 972-529-9700.

Loose Bracket In most cases, a loose bracket will remain attached to the archwire and is not cause for an emergency visit. Sometimes the bracket may move or slide back and forth on the archwire. Tweezers can be used to reposition the bracket if it flips around the wire. If tissue irritation occurs, cover the loose bracket with orthodontic wax to help the sore area heal and call the office soon during office hours to schedule an appointment to fix the bracket.

Call our office if you have questions or to schedule an appointment. An archwire that is not attached to a bracket will not be moving the tooth as it is supposed to. This can result in a delay in treatment progress. A loose archwire needs to be repaired. If a small tie wire causes irritation, push the wire away from the area using the eraser end of a pencil or a cotton swab.

To help the sore spot heal and to let your tongue get used to your braces or appliance, place wax on the area or bracket that is causing the sore spot and use either a warm water rinse or Peroxyl three (3) times daily. Please refer to the topic “Wax” at the bottom of this page for complete instructions on the use of wax. If the wax does not seem to help or if the sore spot gets worse, please call our office.

If the wire cannot be tucked away, cover the end of the wire with a small piece of wax, a cotton ball, or a piece of sugarless gum, until you can see us for an adjustment. Please call our office to schedule an emergency appointment. Loose or Broken Retainers or Removable Appliances The metal or plastic parts of retainers or removable appliances may break or get bent. This can cause them to function improperly. In these situations, it may be necessary to stop wearing the appliance.

Contact us during office hours to schedule an appointment. It may be necessary to schedule an earlier appointment to adjust or repair the appliance. Swallowed Parts If you swallow part of your orthodontic appliance, such as a bracket or rubber band, it will pass through your system and be harmless, unless you experience difficulty breathing. If you experience difficulty breathing, seek immediate medical help. The emergency protocol of Dr. Crise and Dr.

Loose Archwire or Irritating Wire If the main wire has come out of the brace or tube on a back molar tooth, attempt to reinsert the wire with a pair of needle-nosed pliers or tweezers. If the wire is sticking you then you can place a piece of wax over the area. If the wax does not help, the wire can be cut with a small wire cutter or nail clipper close to the back of the last bracket or band. This is the last resort until you can see us for an appointment.

To keep your smile looking its best, you’ll have to wear a retainer to preserve and stabilize your Top McKinney Orthodontics results. Retainers are needed to control or limit potential changes in tooth position. They are used after braces treatment to hold teeth in their correct alignment while the surrounding gums, bone, and muscle adjust to the new positioning of your teeth. Types of Retainers Retainers are custom-made and can be removable or fixed.

If unusual or unexplained symptoms such as swelling or redness in your mouth, or a skin rash appear, please call the Smiles Hollywood office for advice so our trained staff can help determine the origin of the symptoms. After these Top McKinney Orthodontics appointments you will officially be released from our office and there will be an office visit charge of $100 per visit. Retainer Schedule 1st year out of braces: Wear every night** **(Note: In some patients, teeth shifting can occur during the day, therefore, some day wear could be necessary for these patients too. ) 2nd year out of braces: Wear every other night or three times a week.

Retainers are Forever A common misconception is that teeth are like pieces of steel set in concrete—that although the rest of the body changes with age, the teeth will remain straight. The reality is that this is not true. Everything in our bodies changes with age…skin texture, eyesight, hair thickness and color, as well as our bones, which hold our teeth. This is why it is necessary to continue wearing your retainer. This may sound like an unpleasant task, but think about it for a minute.

Bonded Retainer: If your lower teeth are retained with a fixed wire placed on the inside of the front teeth, this should be worn for three years or indefinitely, as long as proper professional Top McKinney Orthodontics cleanings are obtained every six months from the dentist. You should use floss threaders nightly to clean around it properly.

If you are not able to keep the bonded retainer sufficiently cleaned and you are not getting it professionally cleaned regularly then we would not recommend a bonded retainer for you. Retainer Care: Every time you brush your teeth, brush your retainers with cool water and toothpaste. The retainers are heat sensitive and will distort if introduced to warm or hot temperatures.

Traditional removable retainer with a tongue crib , this type of retainer is exactly like the retainer above, however, it has a metal blocking device (like a potato masher) to help keep the tongue from thrusting between the teeth and flaring the front teeth. We usually recommend these for patients who have a tongue thrust habit, in order to decrease relapse. Pros and Cons Removable retainers can be taken out for eating and hygiene routines.

If retainers feel tight when placing them in then go back to wearing the retainers every night. 3rd year out of braces: Wear at least one to two nights a week for the rest of your life. If the retainer feels tight then wear additional nights as needed. A good plan is to pick one day of the week that you will always wear your retainer. We do not recommend wearing your retainer less than one night per week. DO NOT EVER THROW YOU RETAINER AWAY.

If you could sleep on a magic pillow every night to keep your body from changing, would you be willing to do that. If you think of your retainer as “pajamas for your teeth,” then you can keep them straight just by wearing it at night. Retainer Phase: During the next 18 months we will be seeing you approximately five times to check your retainers and evaluate your wisdom teeth. BRING YOUR RETAINERS TO EVERY APPOINTMENT.

Traditional removable retainers typically include a metal wire that surrounds the front teeth and is attached to an acrylic arch that sits in the roof of the mouth. The metal wires can be adjusted to finish treatment and continue minor movement of the front teeth as needed.

Removable retainers can get lost easily, so remember to keep yours in the case whenever you remove it to eat or brush. A fixed retainer is great if you don’t want to keep track of it, or if you don’t want to worry about how many hours per day it must be worn. Teeth with fixed retainers require a little extra attention to remove tartar while flossing. Patients with fixed retainers often must use floss threaders to pass dental floss through the small spaces between the retainer and the teeth.

Aligner-style retainers , or Essix retainers, look similar to clear-aligners and offer a more aesthetic alternative to wire retainers. This clear retainer may fit over the entire arch of your teeth, or only from canine to canine (clip-on retainer). It is produced from a mold of your newly aligned teeth. Fixed retainers consist of wires bonded behind the bottom and/or top teeth.

We, however, offer every patient that we have treated for Braces or clear-aligners at our office one free retainer. We recommend wearing your retainers for as long as you want your beautiful teeth to stay straight. Remember that teeth tend to shift with age. Your Top McKinney Orthodontics retainer is used to help you prevent and control shifting. They say “Nothing is forever,” but we say, “Retainers are forever. ” Keep Smiling.

If you need help choosing the right toothbrush, toothpaste or dental floss, please ask one of our Smiles Hollywood Orthodontic Registered Dental Assistants.

They are always up-to-date on the latest and greatest products for keeping your teeth clean and healthy. Brushing with braces Your work-in-progress Hollywood Smile requires a little bit of extra tender loving care while it is in the transitional phase. Be sure to brush your teeth for two minutes after every meal with a soft-bristled, small-head toothbrush and a fluoride toothpaste.

Please just ask one of our friendly front desk employees for pricing and any questions you may have. Electric Top Mckinney Orthodontics toothbrushes are great for all members of the family to use. We highly recommend them for the entire family. They are super easy to use and take all of the “work” out of brushing your teeth plus they are also fun to use. So, if you are having problems motivating your child to brush their teeth, we highly recommend trying an electric toothbrush.

There are several different brands of electric toothbrushes on the market. There have been several reviews of electric toothbrushes and they show that electric toothbrushes DO decrease more plaque and gingivitis than manual toothbrushes. The rotating toothbrushes seem to work better than just the vibrating toothbrushes. Smiles Hollywood Orthodontics carries them at the front desk, if you are interested.

It puts the “fun” back in brushing teeth. Flossing with braces In addition to brushing your teeth, flossing your teeth while wearing braces can make your mouth feel oh-so-clean. Flossing after every meal will help keep your teeth and braces sparkling, which will also help keep your treatment time on track. Flossing is so very important while wearing braces and/or other orthodontic appliances.

Then, please floss or use a water flosser a minimum of one time per day. It will take more time to do all of these things in the bathroom, but it will be well worth it when you get your braces off and see your beautiful and healthy smile. Smiles Hollywood Top Mckinney Orthodontics is here to help you every step of the way to help our patients have the best possible oral hygiene and the most beautiful Hollywood Smiles.

Particles of food can easily get stuck between your wire and your gum line and if not removed soon, it can cause a gum infection. If your gums do become puffy or red or start to hurt, please let Smiles Hollywood Orthodontics know, and we will get you into our office to irrigate and remove any particles of difficult food. Please don’t let it go too long, so we can get you back on the road to your beautiful smile.

If you are interested in seeing one of them or have any questions, please ask one of our knowledgeable Top Mckinney Orthodontics assistants or one of our friendly front desk specialists. They are happy to tell you all about them. Overall, Brushing and Flossing your teeth is critical and very important to each patient’s orthodontic success. It’s very important to keep your teeth very healthy during orthodontic treatment, since there are so many areas where food particles and bacteria can hide.

A lot of patients will use the water flosser in the shower so that the water sprays out in the shower, rather than all over the counter or mirror above your sink. Moms appreciate this piece of advice. Water flossers really are amazing and are really great for your gums too. There are a few brands of water flossers and we do carry them at our front desk at Smiles Hollywood Orthodontics.

Some patients have asked us if they should brush first or floss first. Dr. Crise recommends that you floss first to remove food and debris and then brush to get around all of the brackets. If you use a water flosser, it often encourages patients to floss even more when they see all the food particles and debris that come out of their teeth and gums. Smiles Hollywood Orthodontics recommends brushing your teeth at least two times per day, but after every meal would be even better.

Gently slide the floss between each set of teeth using a back and forth motion. Floss the sides of each tooth beneath the gum line to remove plaque and food particles. Then you must repeat this same process for each tooth. If you find that using a floss threader is too difficult then ask us about a water flosser. Water flossers are an automated handheld flosser that squirts water out in a steady stream and you spray it between each of your teeth.

It is vital to floss while wearing braces or any Top Mckinney Orthodontics appliance, however, it is more difficult. With braces, one must use a floss threader, which is a special tool to floss under the braces. A floss threader looks like a large plastic threading needle. First you must tie one end of the floss to the needle eye and then use the plastic flexible needle part to put under your wire and floss between the teeth.

Authentic Mexican Tortilla Chips and the like…… The hard pieces will break your braces or get caught in between the wire and your gums. Pretzels (hard) or any hard potato chips (like kettle cooked chips) Bends wires, loosens bands, knocks off brackets. Rib Bones…… Don’t chew on rib bones. They can break off your brackets. Fresh vegetables, fruits and meat Be sure to cut fresh vegetables and fruits, such as carrots and apples, into bite-sized pieces. Do not bite with the front teeth.

You can eat the puffed popcorn that you can find at most grocery stores. After reviewing the No-No-List a patient might think they can’t eat any fun foods during orthodontic treatment. The good news is that there are LOTS of things that you can eat while in braces. Our Top McKinney Orthodontics team at Smiles Hollywood Orthodontics understands just how much our patients enjoy popcorn. Regular popcorn (like you get at the movies or microwave at home) is on the No-No-List.

So, it’s very important to look over this food list to see what you can and can’t eat. There are still MANY yummy foods that you can eat and it’s only for a few years, while you are in Top McKinney Orthodontics treatment. Food for Orthodontic Thought NO-NO-LIST So stay away from things like: Gooey/chewy/sticky These items will pull off your wires and feed the bacteria in your mouth. Suckers/ hard candy……You might weaken your bite and it gets stocks in your braces and wires.

Hard foods Hard rolls, Bagels…… Bends the wires and knocks off the brackets. Ice…. A thousand times NO. It will completely destroy your braces and it’s not good for your teeth in general. Popcorn…. Please stay away from regular popcorn. Please only eat the puffed popcorn, such as Pirate’s Booty or any other kind of hulless popcorn. Pizza crust (hard). The hard crust breaks wires. Nuts…. No nuts of any kind. They get caught in between your wires and gums and can break your wires and/or brackets.

Gum…… no, no, no gum will get stuck in your braces, even the sugar free gum is not good for braces. Caramel candy…. Yikes. Stay away. They are WAY too chewy and gooey. Starbursts…… Please do not eat. They are very chewy and not good for braces. Tootsie Rolls…. Absolutely not, they will get stuck in your brackets. Bagels…. Very chewy and gooey, not good for braces. Beef jerky…… Tough as nails and too chewy. Slim Jims……. Tough as nails and super hard to chew with braces on.

[et_pb_section fb_built=1 fullwidth=on _builder_version=4. 0. 11 global_module=64 saved_tabs=all global_colors_info={}][et_pb_fullwidth_post_title meta=off _builder_version=4. 16 background_image=/wp-content/uploads/mckinney-orthodontics-hero-2.

module_id=no-list _builder_version=4. 16 header_2_font=|||on||||| global_colors_info={}] A few facts about healthy eating while you are in orthodontic treatment Please do not eat hard, gooey, chewy, or sticky foods. Eating these foods can loosen bands, break or bend wires, and detach brackets. You may not discover the problem until hours or days later. If the braces are broken, they cannot move your teeth and this will result in a longer treatment time.

” Loose bands, broken wires and detached brackets. All of which cause a longer Top McKinney Orthodontics treatment time. And remember … never chew on pens or pencils Keep fingers out of your mouth. Limit soft drinks, especially dark colors (due to staining). NO POPCORN (except hull-less) The little kernels get in between the gum and the band. It hurts like crazy and can cause an infection as well as make treatment even longer.

Use the back and side teeth instead. Cut corn on the cob off of the cob. Be sure to cut meat away from the bone. Biting onto a bone like a chicken leg or ribs can break your braces. Cut meat into bite-sized pieces. Never bite into a bone. Some meats may be extremely chewy. Please avoid eating really tough and chewy meats, as these can break off a wire or bracket. Eating these foods will loosen the braces and brackets. However, you may not discover the problem until hours or days later. “Surprise.

4 global_colors_info={}] At Smiles Hollywood Orthodontics, YOU are the star of the show. While you will not need to learn lines or memorize choreography, you will need to do a few special things to take care of your braces during your Top McKinney Orthodontics treatment. Of course, if you ever have any questions about anything, we are always here to help you out and to answer any of your questions. We are on your team and want to help you every step of the way.

You can eat soft tacos, cheese puff balls and thin potato chips Candy Types of candies that are OK include Reeses peanut butter cups, 3 Musketeers, Marshmallows Kit Kats and Hershey bars (NO nuts) Breads Crescent rolls, soft dinner rolls, slice of soft bread and muffins Snacks Pepperoni slices, cheese slices, cut up fruit pieces, cake, pie, cookies (soft ones), puffed popcorn and boiled peanuts Helpful tip: Cut your meat off the ribs and chicken bones The above are just a few items that you should and shouldn’t eat.

However, you can get hulless popcorn, such as Pirate’s Booty, at most grocery stores and big box stores. It comes in butter or cheese flavor and it’s delicious and a great alternative to regular popcorn. We want all of our patients to eat a well balanced diet, including lots of fresh vegetables and fruits. We also know how much our patients like some of the more “fun foods” so listed below are some examples of the kinds of “fun foods” that are OK to eat during your braces treatment.

The Top McKinney Orthodontics team at Smiles Hollywood Orthodontics is always here to answer any questions that you may have. Please ask us about certain foods, if you aren’t sure. If you do break something on your braces or a wire comes loose, please contact us right away, so our team can get you in to fix your braces. If your braces are broken, they aren’t working correctly, so this will delay your treatment time.

Please note that Dr. Crise and Dr. Downs defer to parents when telling patients what they can eat. It is also very important to follow our brushing and flossing guidelines for the best results for a beautiful healthy smile. It is recommended that you use a water flosser, if possible, to help you get into hard to reach areas of your teeth. Also, be sure to use the fluoride rinse every night that we give you at your consultation the day you start your braces. At night time, brush first then swish around a capful of fluoride rinse right before you go to bed and spit it out.

Eating with braces can be a bit challenging at first. There are several things you’ll need to cut up into bite-sized pieces, like apples and carrots. You should never bite into anything that is hard, as this could easily pop off one of your brackets. Also try to avoid foods that are chewy, gooey, hard, and crunchy.

In case of a sports injury, be sure to immediately check your mouth and braces for any damage. If you notice any loose teeth or loose brackets, please contact Smiles Hollywood Top McKinney Orthodontics right away, so we can get you in to have your appliances repaired and to check your teeth. You can temporarily relieve any discomfort by applying wax and rinsing your mouth with warm salt water until we are able to see you.

For general discomfort, we recommend taking an over-the-counter pain relief medication like advil, tylenol or motrin. Soreness after an adjustment is completely normal and should go away within a day or two. On your first visit you should receive a special Top McKinney Orthodontics kit that contains wax that can be used on a pokey wire or bracket until you are able to come into our office to have it repaired.

ACT III: Playing Sports with Braces Wearing a mouthguard during sports activities is essential while you have braces. It will protect your teeth from being injured while you’re playing your sport. If you are in braces, Smiles Hollywood Top McKinney Orthodontics can provide you a simple mouth guard at no charge to help protect your teeth during sports. Please do not buy the boil and bite sports guards from the store, as these can break off your braces.

ACT I: Setting the Stage During your appointment, one of our treatment coordinators will review everything you need to do to achieve a Gorgeous Hollywood Smile. Just follow the directions we give you, and you’ll be on your way to smile stardom. During braces treatment you will need to be diligent with brushing and flossing. Foods can get stuck in brackets and wires, and that doesn’t look good on camera.

Foods such as tootsie rolls, caramels, starbursts, and any other foods that are similar to these, should be avoided, as they get stuck in your wires and brackets and can easily break them. Please also avoid chewing on ice, or pens and pencils. Ice is extremely hard on your teeth and you should really never chew on ice, whether you have braces or not. It’s very important to use common sense on which foods are okay to eat and which are not with your braces.

This may sound like a list of lots of things you cannot eat, but there are so many foods that you still can eat and it’s just a few short years that you will be in braces and all will be well worth it in the end. When your braces come off, feel free to indulge in all the foods you could not have when your braces were on. ACT II: Emergency. If you ever experience an emergency, such as a broken bracket, out of place wire, pokey wire or broken appliance, please do not hesitate to call our office.

Do NOT swallow the fluoride, but always spit it out. The fluoride is to sit on your teeth overnight, as an added protection for your teeth, to prevent decay. Try to not eat or drink anything, (including water) until the next morning. The next morning, you should brush your teeth as normal. If you follow these instructions above, you will be very successful and well on your journey to those perfectly straight healthy teeth and a beautiful Hollywood Smile.

We have a 24-hour emergency line to answer questions and help guide you through whatever discomfort you are feeling. If an office visit is necessary, we will see you as soon as possible. Please do not walk around with a broken appliance or loose bracket for too long, because while your braces are broken they are not effective in moving your teeth until they have been repaired. This can increase your treatment time and keep you in braces longer.

It will take a couple weeks of practicing your instrument for your embouchure to get back to normal again. So, it’s best to leave the Top McKinney Orthodontics braces on for the various competitions and contests until you have a couple weeks to allow your embouchure to re-adjust. We have many band and orchestra students who perform very well with their braces on so this should not be a worry for you.

0 hover_enabled=0 global_colors_info={} sticky_enabled=0] Surgical orthodontics is the combination of orthognathic surgery and Top McKinney Orthodontics braces treatment used to correct patients with misaligned bites, jaw bone abnormalities, and severe malocclusion. Oral and maxillofacial surgery is one of the nine recognized dental specialties, and it focuses on treating complex craniofacial cases that involve the mouth, jaw, face, and skull. If you need surgical orthodontics, Dr.

Stephanie Crise and Dr. Jessica Downs at Smiles Hollywood Orthodontics will work together with an oral and maxillofacial surgeon to ensure that you receive the best care possible. It’s important to have both an orthodontist and an oral surgeon on your dental team because the oral surgeon and the orthodontist must work together to make sure your surgical and orthodontic outcome will be the best it can be. When might surgical orthodontics be needed.

Don’t worry, even the most serious musicians can easily get through braces.

We are often asked if a band or orchestra student should have their braces removed before a big competition, etc. Most band and orchestra instructors would prefer that you do NOT get your braces off right before a big competition or performance. This is because you will have been used to playing your instrument with the braces on and your embouchure will change again once the braces are removed.

As with any major medical surgery, there may be certain risks of having orthognathic surgery. However, the process of orthognathic surgery is not new, and it has been performed for many years in practices and hospitals.

For many patients, the most important reward of having orthognathic surgery is achieving a beautiful, healthy smile that lasts a lifetime. ACTIVE CLEAR INVISIBLE ALIGNERS TAKE YOUR SMILE TO CENTER STAGE BY: Faster treatment: Most Active clear-aligners patients finish twice as fast as clear braces patients. The McKinney clear-aligners treatment is usually between 6 and 12 months. Our trays are different, Smiles Hollywood Orthodontics pride ourselves on custom trays that “track” during the entire treatment.

Convenient: Our Active Clear Aligner treatment is ideal for your busy schedule. Treatment times are usually shorter than with metal or clear braces and it takes less time getting adjustments. Adjustments in our McKinney clear-aligners office are usually about every 8 weeks and the appointments are typically short in duration (less than 30 minutes). Smiles Hollywood Orthodontics knows how important your time is and we strive to make the most of your time.

All cases are looked at by our doctors and a customized treatment plan is made especially for you to give you your best smile. Better fit : Active clear-aligners are comfortable, fit well, and are easy to put on and take off unlike clear braces. Having an in-house laboratory allows the doctors to make adjustments throughout treatment to allow for more comfortable and better-fitting clear-aligners.

Our Active clear-aligners are almost completely invisible and they can simply be taken out of your mouth for those special times that you are “up close and personal” with other people. Most treatments also do NOT require the use of attachments. Why go through with Clear Aligner treatment that is “supposed” to be invisible when most attachments of our competitors are just as visible as clear braces.

While there are some times that attachments must be used for treatment, at Smiles Hollywood Orthodontics we try to make sure that the attachments go on at the end of treatment to minimize the time they are visible. Most of our McKinney clear-aligners are made without those attachments while still moving teeth into their proper positions.

Our Active clear-aligners are a great option for adults with limited time during the day, for professionals that have to leave work, for busy moms and dads, for teens with jammed school and sports schedules, and even for students going back and forth from colleges far away. Appointments are easy, fast, and readily available. To learn more about Smiles Hollywood Orthodontics Active Clear Aligner system and all your orthodontic treatment options, schedule a complimentary appointment in our office.

Effective: Active clear-aligners can treat a wide range of teeth-straightening conditions, including crossbites, crowding, open bites, gapped or spaced teeth, overbites, and underbites. With over 20 years of experience, there are very few types of cases we haven’t treated. Let us wow you with our proven results…” A picture is worth a thousand words.

Comfortable: Our Active clear-aligners are better than clear braces because they are removable so you can continue doing all the things you normally would, from brushing and flossing to eating whatever you like. No metal in your mouth also means no poking wires and no irritated gums. Unlike other Active clear-aligners brands, our’s are custom fit to you and we can even customize them as we go along through treatment.

” Smiles Hollywood McKinney clear-aligners has hundreds of patients that have happily had their smiles transformed with Active clear-aligners over the years and we’re proud to show it. Virtually invisible: Most people won’t even know you’re going through orthodontic treatment because Active clear-aligners are virtually invisible. No need to worry about pictures, dates, speeches, zoom meetings. any activity where you’d be self-conscious about something in your mouth or on your teeth.

Instead of all the aligners being fabricated at one time, patients can get accustomed to the fit and feel of the clear-aligners and give input on how the aligners fit. We can make them tighter or looser, longer or shorter, these trays are customized just for you. Plus, our aligners often do NOT require attachments. Clear aligner trays are removed much easier when attachments are not used and your teeth will still move easily.

Our patients give input to let us know if they need to be tighter, looser, longer, or shorter. Custom fit means comfort. All of our patients love that we don’t regularly use attachments. Smiles Hollywood Orthodontics has been using Active clear-aligners for over 20 years and we have perfected the method of treatment that does not require the use of attachments, and yet we still have the same beautiful results as our competitors.

Using light, gentle forces, In-Ovation makes the path to a beautiful smile faster and more comfortable than you ever thought possible. Traditional Braces Elastics and ties used in older braces put pressure and friction on teeth which slows treatment and causes discomfort. In-Ovation With the In-Ovation self-ligating system, teeth are free to move more quickly, easily and comfortably. A Beautiful Smile is Fast and Easy.

0 hover_enabled=0 global_colors_info={} sticky_enabled=0] In-Ovation® System Braces In-Ovation is a new and technically advanced system that can give you the radiant smile you have always wanted more quickly than traditional braces. Without the need for elastic or uncomfortable metal ties, you have less irritation, less plaque build-up, and less difficulty keeping your teeth clean. Best of all, there’s never a need to have your braces tightened.

0 _module_preset=default column_structure=1_2,1_2 custom_padding=0px||||false|false hover_enabled=0 sticky_enabled=0][et_pb_column _builder_version=4. 21. 0 _module_preset=default type=1_2][et_pb_text _builder_version=4. 21. 0 _module_preset=default hover_enabled=0 sticky_enabled=0 admin_label=before] Before [/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column _builder_version=4. 21. 0 _module_preset=default type=1_2][et_pb_text _builder_version=4. 21.

After reviewing her records, The doctors first recommended sending her to have four premolars removed so that she would not be prone to gum recession. Removing these teeth created space so that her upper canines could move into the proper position in her arch without causing additional flaring of the front teeth. In the lower arch, the removals helped with the alignment of the teeth without causing more flaring.

0 _module_preset=default hover_enabled=0 sticky_enabled=0 admin_label=before] Before [/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column _builder_version=4. 21. 0 _module_preset=default type=1_2][et_pb_text _builder_version=4. 21. 0 _module_preset=default hover_enabled=0 sticky_enabled=0 admin_label=After] After [/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row _builder_version=4. 21.

0 _module_preset=default admin_label= In-Ovation Case Study: ADULT age 56 years old hover_enabled=0 sticky_enabled=0] Smiles Hollywood Orthodontics has done many orthodontic cases with the In-Ovation self-ligating braces, and all of our patients are so happy with their beautiful smiles. Here is an In-Ovation treatment plan that Dr. Crise and Dr. Downs performed on a 14-year-old. She had severe crowding in both arches, as well as thinner gum tissue around her front teeth.

However, there appeared to be a larger amount of space in the upper, so the braces were used to close as much of this spacing as possible. The amount of upper space closure achieved depended on how the upper and lower teeth fit together. We didn’t want the bite in the front to be as tight as it was because there was too much pressure on her front teeth, causing her front teeth to slightly wiggle every time she bit down (fremitus).

When the doctors evaluated her growth film, they could see that she had more of a vertical growth tendency in her lower jaw. This growth pattern made her more prone to having an open bite. By removing the first premolars, this helped the orthodontists to close down her bite during braces treatment. Elastic wear was very important during her treatment. Her good elastic wear helped close down the bite on the sides so that all of the teeth were touching, top to bottom.

Downs performed on a Top McKinney Orthodontics adult patient in her upper 50s. She had full upper and lower In-Ovation self-ligating braces to improve the tooth alignment and resolve the rotations around the lower front teeth. Some bite turbos were added on the very back lower molars to prop her bite off of the front teeth since they were currently mobile and in fremitus. There was some excess spacing in both arches, mainly on the upper left side and the lower left side.

0 hover_enabled=0 global_colors_info={} custom_padding=||0px||false|false sticky_enabled=0][et_pb_column type=4_4 _builder_version=4. 16 global_colors_info={}][et_pb_text _builder_version=4. 21. 0 _module_preset=default admin_label=Smiles Hollywood Orthodontics treats patients of ALL ages hover_enabled=0 sticky_enabled=0] Smiles Hollywood Orthodontics treats patients of ALL ages, including many adults, who get braces. Here is one example of a treatment plan that Dr. Crise and Dr.

This patient was a great cooperator and thus had a beautiful result. In-Ovation Case Study: ADULT age 56 years old [/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row _builder_version=4. 21. 0 _module_preset=default column_structure=1_2,1_2 custom_padding=0px||||false|false hover_enabled=0 sticky_enabled=0][et_pb_column _builder_version=4. 21. 0 _module_preset=default type=1_2][et_pb_text _builder_version=4. 21.

In some patients, lower slenderizing is not enough, however it is necessary for some space to be left open on the upper for composite restorations to be done on the lateral incisors after braces. The upper lateral incisors OR the upper central and lateral incisors can sometimes both need small composites done to make these teeth a little wider in order to close the residual spaces.

Some lower slenderizing was then necessary on her lower teeth to allow for even more of the upper spaces to be closed. Since the lower front teeth were more triangular in shape, doing some lower slenderizing decreased the size of these teeth slightly and made her teeth look better by lessening “dark triangles” by the gumline. The lower slenderizing was enough to allow for full upper space closure.

One of the most challenging aspects of her case was the retention phase after the braces were completed because upper and lower spacing tends to have a high relapse potential. Normally, upper and lower essix retainers are the best for keeping spaces closed since they hold the alignment the tightest and are the best for protecting the teeth against the clenching and grinding habits which this patient did have.

If composites end up being needed, then the doctors would have the patient’s dentist evaluate the Top McKinney Orthodontics spaces before the completion of braces treatment to make sure no further changes were needed before braces were removed. This patient wore elastics (or rubber bands) during the treatment to help achieve the proper bite relationship. The patient’s compliance with elastics was key to a successful Top McKinney Orthodontics treatment finish.

However, this patient wasn’t comfortable wearing essix retainers, so a bonded retainer was another option that was used. Once her braces were removed and a sufficient time had passed for her gums to heal, the doctors did a lower bonded retainer and upper and lower wraparound Hawley retainers. Although these Top McKinney Orthodontics retainers are not quite as reliable as essix retainers in keeping spaces closed, this patient liked them since they didn’t fully cover the teeth.

0 global_colors_info={}] Damon™ Braces Damon Smile Braces Our office is pleased to offer our patients passive self-ligating Damon Smile braces. Damon braces use a slide mechanism to hold the archwire, reducing the amount of pressure exerted on the teeth and allowing the teeth to move more freely, quickly, and comfortably. Thanks to these innovative new braces, achieving your perfect smile can be faster and easier than you ever thought possible. Tie-less braces – a world without elastic ties.

Tie-less braces help reduce the amount of pressure being placed on the tooth and require fewer adjustments because there are no elastics to replace. Damon Smile Damon Smile facilitates healthy tooth movement with complete control. By combining tie-less brackets and high-tech archwires, we can minimize discomfort and create beautiful smiles that last a lifetime. Braces are the beginning, but the system makes the smile.

The Difference is Clear. Damon Clear Damon Clear uses a slide mechanism to maintain archwires within the bracket, allowing them to move freely. This reduces friction and binding, so your teeth can move quickly and more comfortably. Traditional Brackets Traditional braces use elastics, causing friction and pressure that act like parking brakes and can slow down treatment.

That is why we offer a private area in our clinic especially for adults to be seen privately if they prefer. Plus, we work with you to ensure that you receive the most appropriate treatment to meet your individual needs, since there are many options when it comes to orthodontic treatment. We also understand that you have a busy schedule, and we will work with you to make sure each office visit is as convenient as possible.

A bad bite or crooked teeth can cause jaw pain and pressure and/or can lead to decay, chipped or worn teeth and gum disease. Mouths left untreated can get worse — Not receiving orthodontic treatment when it’s needed can create long-term health issues costing you more in the end for uncomfortable periodontal treatment or expensive restorative dental care.

The types of braces that we offer include: Clear Active Aligners Clear/Invisible Braces (Both Damon & In-Ovation self-ligating) Metal Braces (Both Damon & In-Ovation self-ligating) The difference between adult orthodontics and orthodontics for children and teens: Smiles Hollywood Orthodontics recognizes that adults and children have different needs, and require a different level of Top McKinney Orthodontics attention and care.

Treatment options for adults: For many adults, the thought of having metal braces is enough to discourage them from receiving Top McKinney Orthodontics treatment. However, today’s orthodontic treatment options offer a variety of braces and appliances that are comfortable, aesthetic, and customized to meet your needs.

In fact, the American Association of Orthodontists states that one in five orthodontic patients is over the age of 21. Many adults are choosing to receive treatment because they understand the importance of maintaining their Top McKinney Orthodontics health, and they want to feel better about their appearance. Adults everywhere are taking advantage of the opportunity to receive orthodontic care, and now you can too. Why should you consider orthodontic treatment as an adult.

11 global_module=64 saved_tabs=all global_colors_info={}][et_pb_fullwidth_post_title meta=off _builder_version=4. 16 background_image=/wp-content/uploads/mckinney-orthodontics-hero-2. png global_colors_info={}][/et_pb_fullwidth_post_title][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built=1 specialty=on _builder_version=4. 16 global_colors_info={}][et_pb_column type=3_4 specialty_columns=3 _builder_version=4.

Straight teeth are healthy teeth — Teeth that are properly aligned are easier to keep clean with flossing and brushing and may help prevent other health problems. A beautiful, straight Hollywood Smile builds confidence and makes you look and feel younger — Top McKinney Orthodontics treatment can help boost your self-confidence, giving you a better quality of life and the freedom to smile without holding back.

The main difference between treating adults versus treating children and teens is that the jawbones of younger patients are still developing; whereas for adults these bones have stopped growing, which may sometimes make orthognathic surgery necessary in order to align the jawbones. But, don’t let this scare you. Many adults are functioning fine even when the jawbones are not perfectly aligned; we can still get great orthodontic results without aggressive measures like jaw surgery.

We know that starting Braces as an adult can be more challenging than clear-aligners, since you could notice yourself worrying about food stuck in your braces throughout the day. There is an easy fix for this. At our office we provide very small toothpick-sized toothbrushes that are easy to carry with you throughout the day or you can carry a normal toothbrush with you if you prefer. Make sure to brush after every meal to help keep your teeth the cleanest.

This is because your teeth and gums are having to get used to your Braces or clear-aligners and the teeth are being shifted in order to start working towards your new Hollywood Smile. But don’t worry. It is nothing that some Advil or Tylenol can’t help. Your teeth and gums will start to get used to the gentle shifting of your teeth and the discomfort will decrease significantly as time goes on.

With clear-aligners you can easily remove your aligners to brush your teeth and to eat, so many adults prefer this option for those reasons. Adults with Braces do need to avoid certain foods that are chewy, gooey or very hard. These different foods can affect your orthodontic treatment by bending wires or breaking brackets. Broken brackets or bent wires cause treatment to be slowed. If this happens please give us a call as soon as possible so that we can resolve the issue.

This is one of the reasons we at Smiles Hollywood Orthodontics use high performance wires that work over long periods of time, therefore, you will need fewer appointments spaced out about every 6 to 10 weeks (average once every 2 months). These highly desired wires are made with new technology called Nickel Titanium or Niti and are much gentler on your teeth and mouth. We also guarantee extended office hours throughout the week so that we can accommodate your busy schedule.

[et_pb_section fb_built=1 fullwidth=on _builder_version=4. 0. 11 global_module=64 saved_tabs=all global_colors_info={}][et_pb_fullwidth_post_title meta=off _builder_version=4. 16 background_image=/wp-content/uploads/mckinney-orthodontics-hero-2.

All of our assistants are up to date on the latest Top McKinney Orthodontics procedures and appliances. Records Assistant The Records Assistant takes diagnostic records on our Top McKinney Orthodontics patients. These include intraoral pictures, x-rays, impressions and a computerized 3D scan. After the orthodontic diagnostic records are taken, the records assistant prepares charts for the doctor to study the records to create an orthodontic treatment plan.

Our Records Assistant is caring, gentle and loves working with both children & adults. The records assistant also assists in delivering retainers to patients post-treatment and with all of our active Clear Aligner patients. The records assistant needs to be very personable and able to work in a fast paced environment. They have to be able to keep patients on schedule and assist in the flow of the office.

They are responsible for verifying dental insurance before the patients appointment, which entails going into various insurance websites online and entering the patient’s dental insurance information. If this information cannot be found online, our scheduling coordinator will call the insurance company and speak with a representative from that insurance company to find out further information for each specific patient.

These amazing assistants work chairside, with the orthodontist, assisting in orthodontic procedures such as removing and changing wires, fitting bands on teeth, taking impressions, sterilizing instruments, taking x-rays and preparing the patient for the orthodontist. Many of our clinical assistants are Registered Dental Assistants, which means they have taken extra coursework to learn how to be an excellent assistant.

Here are some of the employment positions in our office at Smiles Hollywood Orthodontics: Clinical Assistant Our very caring & gentle Clinical Assistants love working with both children and adults. They are very detail oriented and thorough. They are able to multitask and are very energetic.

After the doctor has gone over the patients diagnostic records and decided on the patients treatment options it is the job of the treatment coordinator to do the consultation with the patient and their parents, if applicable. The treatment coordinator will then go over the treatment plan in detail, as well as the orthodontic insurance with the patient.

This position also coordinates all of the wisdom teeth removal surgeries and other extractions that are done with our traveling dentist who comes to our office. The treatment coordinator ensures that both the medical and dental insurance have been verified for each patient. The treatment coordinator also assists the orthodontist with various other projects, but their main function is to be a patient advocate and act as a liaison between the patient and the doctor.

They collect and enter data into the patient electronic file from the orthodontic exam during the initial patient examination. The treatment coordinator also needs to be a good typist and speller, as they are typing while the doctor calls out orthodontic information for their electronic file.

The marketing directors works off site quite a bit, but comes to the office to mingle with patients and to promote patient contests or promotions that we have going on each month. This person must be tech savvy and have a general idea of how to work with Instagram, Facebook, etc. Outreach and mingling are also crucial to this role.

This person needs to be able to multitask and work at a fast pace, as well as be very personable and quick with math. Marketing Director The Marketing Director is responsible for coordinating marketing outreach for the practice using various avenues of communication including social media, print media, website, contests, events and other means of contact with our patients and potential patients. This person also delivers goodies to our dental partners who work with our practice.

Treatment Coordinator The Treatment Coordinator must have excellent people skills and be able to speak with patients very easily as well as find ways to connect with them. This person works closely with the orthodontist during Top McKinney Orthodontics consultations to obtain vital information from the patient and family about dental and medical history as well as patient orthodontic needs and expected treatment outcomes.

The treatment coordinator also prepares the financial contract for needed orthodontic treatment and goes over all the numbers on the contract with the patient and discusses any questions on the contract that the patient may have. This person also is responsible for following up with any patients who need more time to think about their proposed orthodontic treatment.

The treatment coordinator collects the down payment and helps the patient to set up a payment plan that is good for them, as well as answers any questions that the patient may have. The treatment coordinator is in charge of electronically filing the orthodontic insurance and will follow up with insurance companies when payments are not received in a timely manner.

The scheduling coordinators also handle various correspondence with other dental offices that we partner with while treating our patients. This is a very fast paced job so one could never get bored. This position requires a very detailed oriented person to keep up with all the various information coming in both over the phone and at the front desk.

If something does break in between your appointments, please call our office and we will get you in just as soon as possible. Please don’t walk around with anything broken in your mouth for very long, as your appliances are not working correctly, if things are loose or broken and that will extend your treatment time. No one wants to wear orthodontic appliances any longer than they have to.

Now we realize that you may have come straight from school and may not have had a chance to brush your teeth; but that’s no problem at all. We have prepasted disposable toothbrushes with a special brushing station set up at our office that you are welcome to use each time that you come to your Top Mckinney Orthodontics appointment. Another way to earn Croc Bucks is to have nothing broken in your mouth.

Our patients will receive Crock Bucks every time they come to our office. A successful practice doesn’t just happen; it is the result of a strong Top Mckinney Orthodontics commitment to excellence in the professional community and in the relationships we build with our patients, their families and our colleagues. We always use state of the art equipment and materials for all of our patients to be sure you have the latest and greatest technology in your mouth.

Both of our doctors go to continuing education classes each year, so they can stay on top of the ever evolving art of orthodontics. It’s so important to have an orthodontist who is up to date on everything changing in the field of orthodontics. We appreciate the confidence you have placed in us to provide you with the best orthodontic care possible at Smiles Hollywood Orthodontics, and we thank you for recommending our practice to your friends and family.

When she started her office in Mckinney twenty five years ago, she wanted to combine her passion for Top McKinney Orthodontics with her love of movies and theater to create a place where going to the Orthodontist would be lots of fun. Dr. Crise loves movies and theater and they have been a big part of her life for many years. As a kid, she watched Star Wars 29 times. Now she loves going to movies with her own kids.

Smiles Hollywood Orthodontics loves having fun contests, promotions and we offer many throughout the year. Please be sure that our office has your email and phone number on file, as we will email and/or text out our monthly Top McKinney Orthodontics promotions. We offer a variety of promotions, where you can get entered into contests to win a brand new big tv, airpods or even a night out to the movies with your family.

There is something for everyone who visits Smiles Hollywood Orthodontics. We want everyone of all ages to enjoy themselves while in our office. And behind all this fun is a kid at heart, Dr. Stephanie Crise. Valedictorian of her dental class as well as a lifelong thespian, singer, dancer and mom of three.

We also have events at our office, such as Snow Cone Day where the snow cones are free all day. Dr. Crise has even rented out an entire movie theater for her patients and included a free drink and popcorn for everyone. As an appreciation event for her patients, Dr. Crise has had parties at places like Apex pool/ waterslide park in McKinney, TX as well as Stonebridge Beach club, also in McKinney.

There is also an “Under the Sea” themed section for the little ones with books and toys to allow them to have fun while waiting on older siblings. Of course, there is free wifi, a homework study area and a game station with the latest, cool and exciting video games. Many parents bring their laptops and work in our reception area while waiting for their child to have their orthodontics appointment. Our movie theater, however, is by far a favorite with all of our patients.

From the carpet, to the wall colors to all the decor, the office looks amazing. You would never think that you are at an orthodontist office. The office is filled with life-sized characters of your favorite actors, Spider Man, Yoda, BB8 and many, many more. There is a real movie theater, a hot chocolate/ cappuccino bar for refreshments as well as a slushie machine with two different seasonal flavors. The slushies are a hit with all ages and really help to cool you off on the hot summer Texas days.

When you step into our office, you really do feel like you have walked into a premier event in Hollywood or a very special performance.

Our office loves to have a lot of fun and it is a place where patients are excited to come for every appointment. We look forward to getting to know you better. Giving back to our community is really important to Dr. Crise and our team at Smiles Hollywood Orthodontics. We do this in a variety of ways each year. After Halloween, we have the Candy Buy Back program where our patients can bring any unwanted (sticky) candy to our office.

Our doctors are constantly researching and attending continuing education courses to be sure they are providing top of the line braces treatment, clear aligner treatment, expanders, herbst treatment, retainers and all other orthodontic appliances. Smiles Hollywood Top McKinney Orthodontics wants the very best for all of our patients and that’s why we are continually improving in order to make our office the very best it can be.

Crise’s favorite Top McKinney Orthodontics community outreach is her dental presentations to school kids. Each year, in February (February is Childrens Dental Health month), Dr. Crise and our mascot, Croc Hollywood, go to many schools in the area and teach kids how to brush, floss and keep their teeth shining. Each student goes home with a goody bag that includes a toothbrush, a tooth eraser and some fun items to enjoy.

Effective Straightening: We see all sorts of teeth-straightening issues, including crossbites, crowding, open bites, gapped or spaced teeth, overbites, and underbites. --- Invisible Aligners ---
STRAIGHTEN YOUR TEETH WITHOUT BRACES. The red carpet is ready for your smile’s debut.

Each of them has amazing gifts and talents and adds so much to our dynamic Smiles Hollywood Orthodontic team.
