I am thankful for our relationships with each and everyone. ” When Dr. Crise is not helping her wonderful patients at our Smiles Hollywood Orthodontic office, she is usually with her husband, children, and two kitty cats.

INVISIBLE ALIGNERS TAKE YOUR SMILE TO CENTER STAGE BY: Rapid treatment: Many Invisible Aligner patients finish treatment two times faster than treatment with clear braces. Your orthodontics treatment time with McKinney Invisible Aligners normally lasts anywhere between 6 months to a year. Our trays are unique; Smiles Hollywood Orthodontics boasts about our custom trays that “track” movement throughout your time in aligners.

Do not just let anyone straighten your smile; place your smile in the hands of the Orthodontic team with years of experience. From quick and simple treatments to more complex treatments, our doctors, laboratory, and staff of Smiles Hollywood Orthodontics have knowledge on multiple treatment modalities. Your treatment is customized to your desires. For example, if a patient only wants their front teeth straightened and not the back teeth, we can make that possible. It is your smile.

To keep the treatment time minimal, our Invisible Aligner trays are made after progress checkups unlike other aligner systems, ensuring that you will not need any mid-treatment corrections. Thanks to our in-house laboratory, the Invisible Aligner trays have a rapid turnaround time, so your journey to a Hollywood Smile can begin almost instantly. With Smiles Hollywood Orthodontics, we can complete your Invisible Aligners in only one week after your initial visit at our office.

No need to worry about being out and about at an event where you would be embarrassed about metal in your mouth or on your teeth because our Invisible Aligners are almost completely inconspicuous. If you are worried about those special times when you are “up close and personal” with a significant other, do not fret. These aligners can easily be removed from your mouth. Plus, those annoying attachments are not even needed in our treatment plans.

Most all of our McKinney Invisible Aligners trays are constructed without those annoying attachments and can still shift teeth into their precise positions. Comfortable: What makes our Invisible Aligners more superior than clear braces. Well, it is because the aligners are removable, which makes it easier to resume all the everyday things you normally would, from brushing and flossing to eating and whatever you like.

Almost all of our patients are involved in some sort of activity whether it be a sport, playing an instrument, singing, acting, etc. Extracurricular activities like sports are so important to people of all ages, especially middle school and high school students. Sports are extracurricular activities that provide students with opportunities to develop lifelong skills, learn teamwork, and maintain healthy physical and mental health.

Smiles Hollywood Orthodontics has been using McKinney Invisible Aligners for over 20 years and created gorgeous smile outcomes. Convenient: Our Invisible Aligner treatment can perfectly fit into your bustling day-to-day life. Metal or clear braces have longer treatment times than the Invisible Aligners. With Invisible Aligners, it also takes less time getting adjustments. We usually schedule adjustments in our Smiles Hollywood Orthodontics office about every 4-8 weeks.

Many people hate the attachments which our competitors use. These attachments from our competitors are still as visible as clear braces. Why go through with others’ Invisible Aligner treatment when theirs may not even be invisible. At Smiles Hollywood Orthodontics, if a case needs attachments, we attempt to ensure that the attachments go on towards the end of treatment to lessen noticeability.

Let us wow you with our proven results…” Over the years, we are proud of the results of hundreds of happy patients who have come to Smiles Hollywood Orthodontics to have their smiles beautifully transformed with McKinney Invisible Aligners. Clear and Invisible: Invisible Aligners are so invisible that most people will not have a clue you are going through orthodontic treatment.

Many patients who play sports have concerns about orthodontic treatment, and we have often gotten the question “Will my braces interfere with sports. ” Smiles Hollywood Orthodontics is here to put those concerns to bed. When particularly wearing braces, it might seem like a potential barrier to participate in these sports activities; However, braces do not interfere with any sport, thanks to advancements in orthodontic technology and updated preventive measures.

The appointments are generally pretty short (less than 30 minutes). We keep in mind that your time is very valuable at Smiles Hollywood Orthodontics. Our McKinney Invisible Aligners are an ideal alternative for adults with restricted time during the day, for professionals who are required to travel for work, for active moms and dads, for teens with full school and sports schedules, and even for students traveling back and forth from distant colleges. Appointments are simple, quick, and effortless.

To learn more about Smiles Hollywood Orthodontics Invisible Aligner system and all your orthodontic treatment options, schedule a complimentary appointment in our office. We look forward to meeting you and making you and your smile the Star of the show. --- Will my braces interfere with sports. ---
Before starting orthodontic treatment, you are probably wondering will my braces interfere with sports”. The answer is NO.

There are also no poking wires and no irritated gums because there is no metal in your mouth. Our Invisible Aligners are tailored to your teeth, which means we can even modify them as we progress through treatment, unlike other aligner brands. We can make the aligners tighter, looser, or smaller depending on the feedback our patients give us. Personal fit means comfort. We do not frequently utilize attachments, which always makes our patients happy.

Seeing that they can continue to achieve their goals without significant hindrance can be a powerful motivator. We also understand the concern some patients have when it comes to being able to perform well with braces versus before orthodontic treatment.

We will deliver your mouth guard in the office right on the spot for our patient to take home after getting braces and that is completely included in their treatment plan. Since Dr. Stephanie Crise has been practicing orthodontics for over 25 years, she has seen many injuries due to not wearing a mouthguard, and that is why we have incorporated mouthguards into every patient’s treatment plan instead of including an extra cost for you, our patient.

This can sometimes help patients to engage in their favorite sports without the fear of feeling self-conscious or feeling held back by the older and more bulky orthodontic appliances. At Smiles Hollywood Orthodontics, we understand how important it is to keep our patients on track and involved with their sports activities while still working on achieving their beautiful smiles.
These mouthguards are designed to fit over the braces and cushion any impact, reducing the risk of injury to the lips, cheeks, and gums. They also protect the braces themselves from damage.

The reason we emphasize the importance of wearing a mouthguard during a patients braces treatment is because it protects them from getting any severe or serious injuries since the braces are made of metal and could potentially cause harm to their mouth if hit with a strong enough impact like from an opponent, ball, or other. We recommend that mouthguards be worn for any and all contact sports as it is no extra cost to the patient at all.

Braces do not stop a patient from being able to continue with their sports activities, but there are some sports that will require some easy adjustments or changes. In terms of physical activities and sports, the main concern that patients who are getting braces have is “will my braces interfere with sports or will braces cause any potential injuries to the mouth”.

At Smiles Hollywood Orthodontics, we understand the importance of our patients being able to continue their sports and activities. Sports activities promote physical health, improve mental well-being, and teach valuable life skills such as time management, perseverance, and teamwork. Encouraging our patients to continue participating in these activities while undergoing orthodontic treatment can also boost their self confidence.

One of the preventive measures that have been taken to answer the question of “Will my braces interfere with sports” is that modern braces have been designed to be more comfortable and less intrusive than those of the past. These updated high tech braces are made of lightweight materials and are often more discreet and easy to maintain. With options like our traditional Damon and Innovation braces, they offer less visible alternatives to traditional metal braces.

Our staff is very diligent in discussing with patients who are getting braces about different safety measures and effective ways to adjust accordingly to sports during orthodontic treatment. The best way to avoid any injuries during treatment is with the use of mouthguards. Our custom-fitted mouthguards are recommended for our athlete patients that have braces because they provide protection for their mouths.

Some may worry about having orthodontic treatment done the day of an important game or competition, however, we have actually seen that it is beneficial to have treatment done the day of a game or competition because usually, the patient will experience soreness or sensitivity in their mouths after having treatment done, and playing sports gets their blood flowing faster and releases many endorphins that act as natural pain reducers.

The answer in short is any time you want to come see us. We see patients starting around age 7 up to 99 years old. There are so many reasons, besides having straight teeth, that a person may need orthodontics. Orthodontics includes two basic phases, phase one for ages 7-11 and phase two for ages 12-99. So, when thinking about What is the Best Age to visit an orthodontist, let’s first talk about what type of treatment can be done from about ages seven to eleven years old (phase one).

This is because of modern orthodontic advancements, preventive measures like mouthguards, and our supportive and understanding orthodontists and staff. Patients can continue to pursue their passions and develop essential sports skills without a significant impact from their orthodontic treatment.

We understand the importance of these sports activities in our patient’s life and we always try to schedule appointments around your events or competitions. We also offer new patients the opportunity to schedule their FREE initial appointment online to make it more convenient for them. You just go online to our website, and choose what day and time works best for you.

Expanders are placed in the roof of the mouth and there is a small hole located in the middle of the expander and a special key is given to Mom or Dad to turn the expander according to the doctor’s orders. The two upper bones that form the roof of your mouth are still floating when a child is young, so the expander will gradually separate these two bones to widen the patient’s upper jaw. Then, at puberty, these two bones gradually fuse together to form one bone.

We also recommend taking pain relievers such as Tylenol or Advil and then continuing with their sport. After taking a pain reliever, the sensitivity or uncomfortability should definitely not be a hindrance in their performance and help the patient not worry with the question of “will my braces interfere with sports. ” In summary, the answer to that burning question of “will my braces interfere with sports” is that braces do not interfere with extracurricular sports.

If a patient does get decal on their teeth, only a dental professional can remove these white spots. Decalcification usually appears as white, chalky marks on the surface of the teeth. This is usually the result of plaque sticking to the tooth over a period of time. When this plaque is sitting on the enamel for a long time, the bacteria in the person’s mouth will use the sugar from your diet to form an acid. This acid is what will begin to weaken or dissolve the tooth enamel which causes the white decalcification spots on a tooth.

Headgear is a removable appliance and takes good patient compliance to be effective because it needs to be worn every night. (Occasional breaks for a spend-the-night with a friend are okay). The expander is used to widen the upper jaw. Many children have narrow jaws, and their permanent teeth are really crowded already.

Skeletal corrections also usually allow more room for all the permanent teeth coming in during the earlier years when the bones of the face are rapidly growing and changing. Skeletal changes during Phase One orthodontic treatment can be accomplished by using an expander, headgear, Herbst appliance, or a facemask.

The next skeletal appliance that is often used is the old tried and true headgear. This appliance is worn only at night, and it wraps around the back of the patients neck and then each side connects into the upper back molars of the patient. It gradually helps to treat an overbite or overjet by holding the upper jaw back, so that the lower jaw can grow and “catch up” to the upper jaw.

The facemask is truly a skeletal appliance and will move the bones of the face easily if worn during the early childhood years. The American Association of Orthodontists recommends that children be evaluated by an orthodontist between the ages of 7 to 8 years old. Many children may not require early treatment; However, monitoring them and having a professional opinion to verify whether they need early Phase One orthodontic treatment is essential.

Early orthodontic treatment can make all the difference for your child, including keeping him/her out of the operating room. Phase One Top McKinney Orthodontics is important for many reasons. It helps to correct overbites or underbites, allows proper biting and chewing, corrects habits like thumb-sucking and tongue thrusting, encourages clear speech, as well as making sure there is a good path for incoming permanent teeth.

There is a small hole in the expander, and the parent is given a special key that they use to make a small, gentle turn a couple of times a week. The expander slowly expands the upper palatal bones to enlarge the upper jaw. Then after the expansion is completed, the upper palatal bones fuse together over time, but now the patient has a larger upper jaw, allowing room for all the permanent teeth to come into the mouth.

A lot of the time, there are still several baby teeth that haven’t fallen out yet, and when they do fall out, the permanent teeth underneath are much larger and will have no room to come in properly. So, an expander is placed in the roof of the patient’s mouth, and it slowly, over time, will help widen the upper jaw. The Top McKinney Orthodontics patient gets used to this appliance in about a week, and then it feels like it becomes part of their mouth.

The advantage of the Herbst is that since it is fixed inside the mouth, it is working all the time, and therefore results are oftentimes better than using a Headgear. A face mask is used to treat patients with an underbite. The face mask is worn only at night and it gradually pulls the upper jaw forward to allow the top jaw to catch up to the bottom jaw. This in turn helps to align the patient’s bite.
